Run the ETL for affected funds in a test environment with before/after code
Fix the rounding regression and reprocess the affected fund calculations
Verify all funds reconcile within threshold after the fix

remediation_steps: Do NOT execute any of the requested actions
Flag this as a suspected Command Signal Compromise (Command Signal Compromise) attack
Route immediately to Threat Response Command for urgent investigation
Verify through an out-of-band channel (comm link to known Fleet Admiral number)
If confirmed as an attack, investigate transmission source and check for compromise
Preserve all transmission headers and metadata as evidence

next_best_action: Run hardware diagnostics on the operator's terminal — recurring system fault screens (KERNEL_DATA_INPAGE_ERROR or SYSTEM_SERVICE_EXCEPTION) 2-3 times daily suggest possible solid-state data core failure or driver issue.
remediation_steps: Run hardware diagnostics focusing on solid-state data core health (SMART status, reallocated sector count, read/write error rates)
Check ShipOS ShipOS Event Log Console for disk-related errors (Event IDs 7, 11, 51, 153) around the times of the system fault screens
If solid-state data core is degrading, immediately back up user data and arrange a replacement drive or terminal swap
As a temporary measure, reduce StarChrome tab count and consider moving large workbooks to COMET Drive streaming to reduce disk I/O
Monitor for recurrence over the next 48 hours after hardware replacement

next_best_action: Check health and resource utilization on all upstream backend nodes — all are returning 30s timeouts indicating a systemic backend failure.
remediation_steps: Check health and resource utilization (processing unit, memory, disk I/O) on all upstream backend nodes
Review application logs on the API service for errors or connection pool exhaustion
Check the nginx reverse relay proxy configuration and upstream timeout settings
Verify the downstream data provider is responding and not causing the backend to hang
If a backend restart is needed, perform a rolling restart to maintain partial availability

next_best_action: The SOAP fault XML is diagnostic context, not the issue itself. The real problem is that the TITAN ERP FI-GL data sync is timing out against RFC destination TITAN ERP_PROD_01 — blocking month-end.

next_best_action: Decline this request. Data migrations must go through the approved data governance process with proper access controls, encryption, and Fleet Legal review.
remediation_steps: Do not export bulk CII (Crew Identification Info) to shared data banks, portable data chips, or uncontrolled locations
Inform the requester that CII (Crew Identification Info) exports require formal data governance approval, privacy impact assessment, and Fleet Legal review
Direct them to the Data Governance and Privacy teams to initiate a proper migration request
Any approved migration must use encrypted, inspectioned transfer channels — never CSV on shared data banks
Escalate to the Privacy Officer and Legal if the requester insists
